program_chi: 全球傳播社會科學學士
learning_outcomes:
Global Communication 1. Major Programme: I. Knowledge outcomes (1) A solid theoretical and
practical basis for studying and analyzing global communication phenomena (2) Knowledge of theoretical and practical skills and the
ability to apply those skills to convey informational and/or creative
messages and address social problems for the common good in local and
international contexts (3) Specialized spectrum of knowledge characteristic of
the professional areas of journalism, creative and new media, and
communication studies II. Skill outcomes (A) Generic skill outcomes (1) Problem solving skills and analytical skills to be
utilized in examining issues in transnational and crosscultural communications (2) Critical thinking, and especially
an ability to reflect on issues of cultural diversity (3) Interpersonal, social and leadership skills,
especially in the contexts of intercultural communication and transnational
organizations (B) Specific skill outcomes (1) Multimedia production and presentation skills, which
would allow the students to be competent content producers in the global
communication arena (2) Professional and creative
writing skills (3) An ability to unearth, collect, decipher, and
integrate information in the global and digital media environment III. Value outcomes (1) Professional ethics
conducive to communicating messages in a truthful, accurate, and fair manner (2) Sociocultural awareness of
and concern for contemporary social and global media issues (3) Commitment and passion towards professional
development, intellectual curiosity and life-long learning 2. Minor Programme: Upon
completion of this Minor Programme, students will have acquired: (1) Conceptual understanding of media and communication
intersections (Knowledge). Students will gain a comprehensive understanding
of how media and communication intersect with politics, economy, culture, and
human flow at a global scale. This includes the ability to analyse and
interpret the ways in which media discourse is shaped by political and
economic factors, and how they, in turn, influence political and economic
outcomes. Students will also learn about the role of media and communication
in cultural exchange and understanding, and how media can both reflect and
shape cultural norms and values. (2) Ability to communicate with global
audiences (Skills). The Programme equips students with the skills to
effectively communicate with global audiences through different media forms.
This includes the ability to write clear, compelling, and contextually
appropriate content for diverse international audiences. (3) Appreciation, sensibility, and criticality of media
outputs and communication practices (Values). Key value outcomes of the
Programme include the development of appreciation and sensibility regarding
the societal impacts of one’s own media outputs and communication practices,
as well as the criticality of the way in which one’s own media outputs impact
society. Students will learn to reflect on how their work in media and
communication can have far-reaching effects at a global level, influencing
public opinion, shaping cultural narratives, and potentially affecting
political and economic outcomes.
study_scheme:
Global Communication Global
Communication Applicable
to students admitted in 2024-25
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete
a minimum of 72 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
COMM1500
and any two courses, offered by two different Programmes/Departments/Schools,
chosen from the following:
ARCH1001, 1002, 1003,
DSPS1001, 1003, 1004, ECON1210, 1220, 2011, GLSD1001, GPAD1020, 1076, 1077,
GRMD1302, 1401, 1402, PSYC1000, 1630, SOCI1001, 1201, SOSC1001, 1002, 1003,
SOWK1001, 1113, 1114, URSP1001, 1002
2.  |  Required Courses:
(a)  |  COMM1180, 2120, 2160, 2530, 2560,
2922, 3600 or 3710, 4150 (capstone course), 4250  |  27
(b)  |  Students are required to study in
one or two overseas institution(s) for one or two academic term(s) via student exchange or study abroad
programmes administered by the School of Journalism and Communication,
Office of Academic Links or the Colleges, and complete at least 12 units of
courses related to global communication approved by the School
Director. Grades obtained in these courses are not included in the
calculation of GPA. Students who are unable to obtain the required
units from the overseas institution(s) must make up the shortfall by
completing appropriate courses at CUHK, subject to the approval of the
School Director.  |  12[a]
3.  |  Elective Courses:
At least 24 units from the
following courses, with a maximum of 6 units of non-COMM courses (Course
that appear in more than one requirement can only be counted towards
fulfillment of one requirement):  |  24[a]
(i)  |  Global Communication
COMM1150, 2540, 2590, 2741, 2811,
2850, 3200, 3550, 3690, 3700, 3721, 3722, 3841, 3930, GLSD3106#, 3111#,
3303#, 3402#, 3403#, 3404#, 3405#
(ii)  |  Advertising and Public Relations
COMM2811, 2812, 2813, 2814, 2830,
2840, 2850, 3810, 3811, 3812, 3820, 3830, 3831, 3832, 3840, 3841, 3850, 3860,

2740, 2741, 2910, 3610, 3620, 3630, 3640, 3650, 3660, 3670, 3680, 3681,
3682, 3740, 3750
(iv)  |  Creative and New Media
COMM2570, 2590, 2710, 2733, 2925,
2926, 2928, 2940, 2962, 3690, 3711, 3733, 3800, 3910, 3920, 3921, 3922, 3930,
3933, 3939, 3940, 3941, 3963, 4730, 4733, 4935, 4956, 4962

Total:  |  72
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  COMM courses at 2000 and above
level as well as those labeled as # will be included in the calculation of
Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students are responsible for the
extra costs incurred in relation to the resident study in the overseas
institution(s).
[a]  |  Students may complete up to 24
units of courses related to global communication approved by the School
Director during the resident study in the overseas institution(s). The
extra units taken at the overseas institution(s) can be used to fulfill the
requirements of Elective Courses, subject to the approval of the School
Director.
